name: CMB large-scale anomalies (low-l power, axis of evil, hemispherical asymmetry, cold spot)
requirement: Any candidate framework must accommodate (or predict) the persistent ~2-3 sigma anomalies in the large-scale CMB temperature pattern observed by WMAP and confirmed by Planck. These include: (a) low quadrupole and octopole power; (b) alignment between the quadrupole and octopole and special directions ('axis of evil'); (c) hemispherical power asymmetry; (d) the Cold Spot in the southern Galactic hemisphere; (e) lensing-amplitude tension A_L = 1.18 +/- 0.06.

regime.description: Last-scattering surface at largest angular scales, multipoles l ~ 2-30; horizon-scale features.

current_bound.value: Each individual anomaly at ~2-3 sigma. Combined a posteriori significance debated.
current_bound.units: various; significances quoted in standard deviations
current_bound.reference: Planck Collaboration, 'Planck 2018 results. VII. Isotropy and statistics of the CMB', A&A 641, A7 (2020), arXiv:1906.02552
current_bound.confidence_level: Each at ~2-3 sigma; cumulative significance is contested due to a-posteriori-statistics concerns

what_it_excludes: Anomalies at the few-sigma level are easy to dismiss as look-elsewhere artifacts (the multitude of CMB statistics each yields some 2-3 sigma feature by chance). However, their persistence across WMAP and Planck and their broad spatial coherence motivate non-standard explanations: anisotropic inflation, broken statistical isotropy, topology, residual foregrounds, or non-Gaussian initial conditions. Any framework predicting deviations from statistical isotropy on the sky must score against these features.

parameterization.name: Standard inflationary perturbation theory predicts statistical isotropy; deviations parameterized by (a) multipole-by-multipole power-suppression factor S(l), (b) dipolar modulation amplitude A and direction, (c) bipolar spherical-harmonic moments B_LM^ll'.
parameterization.reference: Schwarz, Starkman, Huterer, Copi, Phys. Rep. 778-779, 1 (2018), arXiv:1510.07929; Hanson and Lewis, PRD 80, 063004 (2009).
parameterization.summary: Quantitative measures of each anomaly (low power, alignment, asymmetry, cold-spot statistics) all yield numerical statistics with calculable distributions under LambdaCDM null.

procedural.predicted_observable: Statistical anisotropy measures: angular power suppression at low l; multipole-vector alignments; dipolar-modulation amplitude.
procedural.bound_in_parameterization: Each anomaly quantified by its specific statistic; LambdaCDM is consistent with observation at the 2-3 sigma per-anomaly level.
framework_inputs: If the framework predicts an anisotropic or topologically non-trivial early universe, compute the modified angular power and multipole alignments.
Include foreground systematics (Galactic dust, synchrotron) when comparing predictions to data.

primary_references.citation: Planck Collaboration, 'Planck 2018 results. VII. Isotropy and statistics of the CMB', A&A 641, A7 (2020)
Schwarz, Starkman, Huterer, Copi, 'CMB Anomalies after Planck', Phys. Rep. 778-779, 1 (2018)
